summary: No-ship: R-1’s formatting defects are fixed, but its second release-profile result remains unauditable as a fresh machine capture.

findings.title: Missing verification: R-1 reuses the rejected release transcript
findings.body: The second command omits Cargo’s `running 2 tests` preamble, contradicting the claimed untruncated raw capture. After normalizing only `[exit=0]`, lines 272–276 are byte-for-byte identical to the round-4 manual block, including the 0.28s duration. No raw capture, generator, invocation record, or SHA-stamped provenance was committed, so the sole release-profile control still cannot be distinguished from reused manual text. The stale `/tmp` reference, duplicate header, commands, exits, and fences are otherwise corrected.
findings.recommendation: Commit a script-owned record keyed to green SHA b2d0f31 containing both exact commands, complete stdout/stderr—including both test-count preambles—and numeric exits, then generate verification.md from that record.

next_steps: Replace only the R-1 evidence and repeat the scope-pinned R-1 audit; do not rerun or revise R-2 through R-4.
